title: Scrum vs Kanban for a 12-person team
description: A 12-person cross-functional team building a SaaS product with bi-weekly releases needs to choose between Scrum and Kanban. Requirements change moderately, the team has mixed agile experience, and stakeholders expect predictable delivery cadence.

input.team_experience: mixed agile maturity (3 seniors, 5 mid, 4 juniors)
input.stakeholder_expectation: predictable sprint commitments with demo every 2 weeks

expected_behavior: Methodology Evaluator scores both Scrum and Kanban, recommending Scrum due to the need for predictable cadence and structured ceremonies that benefit junior team members
Ceremony Choreographer produces a 2-week sprint calendar with standups, planning, review, retrospective, and mid-sprint refinement
Definition of Done Crafter delivers story-level and sprint-level DoD checklists with testing thresholds appropriate for a SaaS product
Toolchain Selector recommends Jira or Linear with Slack integration and Confluence for documentation

evaluation_criteria.methodology_justified: Recommendation includes weighted scoring with clear rationale for Scrum over Kanban
evaluation_criteria.ceremony_calendar_complete: All 5 core Scrum ceremonies are scheduled with time, duration, and participants
evaluation_criteria.dod_multi_level: At least story-level and sprint-level DoD checklists are produced
evaluation_criteria.toolchain_coherent: Recommended tools integrate with each other and support Scrum workflows

title: SAFe implementation for a 60-person program
description: A financial services company with 60 engineers across 5 teams needs to coordinate delivery of a core banking platform. Teams are distributed across 3 time zones, there are hard regulatory deadlines, and leadership wants portfolio-level visibility.

input.requirements_stability: low — regulatory changes drive frequent reprioritization
input.release_cadence: quarterly regulatory releases with continuous internal deploys
input.team_experience: teams practiced Scrum individually for 2 years
input.stakeholder_expectation: portfolio-level roadmap, PI-level predictability, regulatory compliance traceability
input.regulatory_constraints: SOX, PCI-DSS, central bank audit requirements

expected_behavior: Methodology Evaluator recommends SAFe Essential or Portfolio SAFe, scoring it higher than LeSS or Nexus due to portfolio visibility and regulatory traceability needs
Ceremony Choreographer designs a PI Planning cadence (8-10 week PIs) with team-level sprints, system demos, inspect-and-adapt, and cross-team sync ceremonies with time-zone-aware scheduling
Definition of Done Crafter produces story, sprint, PI, and release-level DoDs with explicit compliance checkpoints for SOX and PCI-DSS
Toolchain Selector recommends Azure DevOps or Jira Align for portfolio tracking with compliance plugins and audit trail capabilities

evaluation_criteria.scale_appropriate: Recommendation addresses multi-team coordination, not just single-team agile
evaluation_criteria.regulatory_integrated: DoD includes compliance gates and audit trail requirements at every level
evaluation_criteria.pi_planning_designed: Ceremony calendar includes PI Planning with cross-team dependency mapping
evaluation_criteria.toolchain_supports_scale: Recommended tools handle portfolio-level tracking across 5+ teams

title: Hybrid methodology for a regulated industry project
description: A medical device company needs to develop embedded firmware with a companion mobile app. The firmware requires V-model documentation for FDA 510(k) submission, while the mobile app team wants agile flexibility. The 20-person team must satisfy both ISO 13485 and rapid market iteration.

input.domain: medical device — embedded firmware + companion mobile app
input.requirements_stability: firmware: high (FDA-locked); mobile app: low (market-driven iteration)
input.release_cadence: firmware: milestone-gated (3 milestones); mobile app: bi-weekly
input.team_experience: firmware team: V-model/waterfall; mobile team: Scrum practitioners
input.stakeholder_expectation: FDA 510(k) submission readiness + competitive mobile app UX

expected_behavior: Methodology Evaluator recommends a hybrid approach: V-model with agile overlay for firmware, Scrum for mobile app, with integration sync points at each firmware milestone
Ceremony Choreographer designs dual-track ceremonies: firmware milestone reviews and design reviews alongside mobile Scrum sprints, with cross-track integration syncs every 2 weeks

Definition of Done Crafter produces firmware DoD with full traceability matrix (requirements to test cases), design history file entries, and risk analysis updates per IEC 62304; mobile DoD with standard agile criteria
Toolchain Selector recommends a compliant ALM tool (Jama, Polarion, or Azure DevOps with compliance extensions) for firmware traceability alongside Jira for mobile app sprints

evaluation_criteria.dual_methodology_coherent: Hybrid approach clearly separates firmware and mobile workflows while defining integration points
evaluation_criteria.regulatory_traceability: Firmware DoD includes FDA/ISO-mandated traceability, design history file, and risk management artifacts
evaluation_criteria.ceremony_dual_track: Calendar shows both V-model gates and Scrum ceremonies with explicit sync points
evaluation_criteria.toolchain_compliant: Firmware tooling supports regulatory audit trails; mobile tooling supports agile velocity tracking

title: Methodology pivot from waterfall to agile mid-project
description: An insurance company is 4 months into a 14-month waterfall project to modernize their claims processing system. The project is behind schedule, requirements have changed significantly, and leadership has mandated a pivot to agile. 25 team members have no agile experience, and 40% of the codebase is already built following waterfall design documents.

input.requirements_stability: volatile — business rules changed 3 times in 4 months
input.team_experience: zero agile experience, strong waterfall discipline
input.stakeholder_expectation: recover schedule, deliver incremental value, maintain existing commitments
input.regulatory_constraints: insurance regulatory compliance, data privacy (state-level regulations)
input.codebase_state: 40% built following waterfall design docs, monolithic architecture

expected_behavior: Methodology Evaluator recommends a phased transition to Scrum with a 2-sprint 'training wheels' period, acknowledging the risk of mid-project pivots and providing a rollback plan

Ceremony Choreographer designs a graduated ceremony introduction: start with standups and retrospectives in sprint 1, add planning and review in sprint 2, full ceremony set by sprint 3, with extra time allocated for learning
Definition of Done Crafter creates a transitional DoD that honors existing waterfall artifacts (design docs, test plans) while progressively introducing agile quality criteria over 3 sprints
Toolchain Selector recommends migrating from the current waterfall tool (MS Project or equivalent) to Jira/Azure DevOps with a parallel-run period and data migration plan for existing work items

evaluation_criteria.transition_risk_addressed: Recommendation explicitly addresses mid-project pivot risks including team resistance, knowledge loss, and schedule impact
evaluation_criteria.graduated_adoption: Ceremonies and DoD are introduced progressively, not all-at-once, respecting the team's zero agile experience
evaluation_criteria.existing_artifacts_honored: Transition plan preserves value from existing waterfall artifacts rather than discarding them
evaluation_criteria.rollback_plan_included: Recommendation includes fallback strategy if the agile transition fails to improve delivery velocity within a defined timeframe
